scope.window: recent memory and review records from 2026-07-05 through 2026-07-06
scope.sampling_rule: seed entries are hand-selected from recent high-signal records; this is not a full scan of all closeout receipts or all governance outputs

claim_ceiling: classifies recent evidence of decision effects
does not remove, retire, merge, downgrade, or enforce any defense
does not prove overall governance quality
does not prove every relevant decision-changing output was found

qualifies_when: a later decision or action changes because of that output
the decision or action has a cited evidence reference
does_not_qualify_when: a report was generated but no later action is evidenced
a tool exists but no load, call, or review path is evidenced
a duplicate rule exists only in a sidecar artifact that readers do not load

output_line.purpose: classify defenses that produced visible outputs

inventory_line.purpose: make zombie candidates visible by comparing a full defense inventory against observed outputs

frequency_class.routine: Expected to produce frequent operational output.
frequency_class.rare_critical: Expected to fire rarely but carry high consequence; do not retire solely because it has no recent output.
frequency_class.unknown: Frequency expectation has not been classified.

rare_critical_policy.rule: Human-only gates, safety boundaries, destructive-operation checks, and escalation surfaces must not be retired only because they did not fire in the sampled window.
required_evidence_for_retire: inventory-line evidence that the defense is unreachable or superseded
a focused review of its safety role

classification_rules.decision_changing: Output has evidence of changing scope, implementation, validation, claim ceiling, release note, review verdict, commit, push, or next slice.
classification_rules.decision_relevant_unacted: Output identifies a plausible issue, but the record shows owner decision or follow-up is still pending.
classification_rules.noisy: Output fires or appears in artifacts but current evidence does not show it changed a decision.
classification_rules.zombie: Defense exists in the full inventory but has no recent load, call, report, or decision path. This requires the inventory_line; output_line data alone cannot prove zombie status.
classification_rules.duplicate: Same rule or guidance appears in multiple surfaces and should be merged around a canonical source before retirement.
classification_rules.unknown: Evidence is insufficient to classify safely.

recommended_action_rules.keep: Retain as-is or continue current use.
recommended_action_rules.keep_observe: Retain, but gather more evidence before promoting or retiring.
recommended_action_rules.merge: Consolidate into a canonical source or summary before changing status of source notes.
recommended_action_rules.downgrade: Keep visible but lower prominence, cadence, or claim strength.
recommended_action_rules.retire_candidate: Candidate for retirement after focused review; not retired by this seed.
recommended_action_rules.investigate: Open a read-only slice before changing behavior.

entries.observed_output: Review found false-green positive/negative fixture classification and placeholder misclassification risks.
entries.rationale: The review finding caused a focused bugfix commit and new regression coverage.
entries.claim_ceiling: Proves this diagnostic changed a bugfix decision, not that test suites are industry-grade.

entries.observed_output: Dogfood found fixture association false negatives in representative contract repos.
entries.rationale: Dogfood output led to fixture_manifest mapping, safe aliases, ambiguous-match reporting, and focused tests.
entries.claim_ceiling: Fixture association accuracy improved; validator execution remains unproven.

entries.observed_output: Review found manifest shape crash risk, expected_rule_ids fallback gap, and missing runner visibility.
entries.rationale: The output produced report v0.2, defensive parsing, fixture_runner visibility, cannot_claim refinement, and four pin tests.
entries.claim_ceiling: Report-only diagnostic hardening only; no validator execution or enforcement claim.

entries.observed_output: Test-quality audit line showed TESTING.md doctrine was not visible at consumer implementation time.
entries.rationale: The governance output caused semantic test-quality expectations to be moved into consumer-facing managed instruction surfaces.
entries.claim_ceiling: Instruction visibility only; does not guarantee agent compliance or industry-grade tests.

entries.observed_output: Enumd hook smoke showed manual_update_advisory failed from hook path due to PYTHONPATH import context.
entries.rationale: The hook smoke changed implementation by preserving PYTHONPATH around the pre-commit advisory call.
entries.claim_ceiling: Hook import reliability only; advisory remains report-only.

entries.observed_output: R3 evidence durability bypass and CI warning parity gap were identified.
entries.rationale: The output led to a bypass fix and CI surfacing of test_evidence_provenance_not_found warnings.
entries.claim_ceiling: Warning parity and durability hardening only; evidence truth is not proven.

entries.observed_output: Policy-only blockers could leave completion_claim_allowed inconsistent.
entries.rationale: The finding changed completion-claim behavior so policy-only blockers make completion_claim_allowed false.
entries.claim_ceiling: Completion claim consistency only; does not prove memory content quality.

entries.observed_output: Non-daily session-shaped memory files were outside daily-file B0 scan.
entries.rationale: The residual produced a design slice and later a report-only warning detector without changing blocking policy.
entries.claim_ceiling: Report-only visibility; F6 blocking and bypass-proofing remain unclaimed.

entries.output_type: strict fixture expectations and warning parity
entries.observed_output: Disable-policy attestation gaps were pinned by xfail fixtures, converted to report-only warnings, and surfaced across CI/workflow/pre-commit.
entries.rationale: The sequence shows report-only outputs changing implementation while preserving blocker semantics.
entries.claim_ceiling: Visibility and parity only; no true authorization or prevention proof.

entries.observed_output: Docs/governance classification exposed active, superseded, historical, and proposal-only notes.
entries.rationale: The classification artifact drove retrieval-authority and cache-aware consolidation slices.
entries.claim_ceiling: Classification guides consolidation; it does not itself remove or invalidate documents.

entries.observed_output: Retrieval-authority notes were consolidated into a Round A summary and source-note pointers.
entries.rationale: The duplicate cluster changed reader routing but source notes were intentionally preserved.
entries.claim_ceiling: Human-visible consolidation only; source notes are not retired by this entry.

entries.observed_output: Cache-aware design notes were consolidated into Round B summary metadata and source-note pointers.
entries.rationale: The duplicate cluster changed reader routing but intentionally avoided archive/delete/frontmatter changes.
entries.claim_ceiling: Consolidation pointer only; no source note is removed, archived, invalidated, or reclassified.

entries.observed_output: Review concluded consolidation only partly answers which defenses can merge and that decision-change measurement is the deeper priority.
entries.rationale: The review directly produced this decision-change ledger seed slice.
entries.claim_ceiling: Planning evidence only; this seed does not prove effectiveness of all defenses.

entries.observed_output: External repo live verification found failing smoke outcomes and corrected a prior overclaim about raw evidence.
entries.rationale: The output changed the claim ceiling and produced owner-decision follow-ups, but the owner decisions are still pending.
entries.claim_ceiling: Read-only external verification only; does not repair consumer repos.

entries.observed_output: Unregistered governance documents are surfaced as warning-only output instead of being silently skipped.
entries.rationale: The warning surface has driven authority registration work and currently reports 13 remaining unregistered documents. The exact 16-to-13 historical delta is review-derived and should be backed by a persisted ledger entry before using it as quantitative proof.
entries.claim_ceiling: This seed treats the warning as decision-changing but does not claim all authority-visible docs are registered.

entries.observed_output: Lock-vs-checkout consistency is visible as a first-class summary row and catches CFU-style drift.
entries.rationale: The field exists specifically to prevent claiming completed/latest governance when framework.lock.json and the checked-out framework differ.
entries.claim_ceiling: Local comparison only; no fetch truth or full adoption proof.

entries.observed_output: Latest closeout receipt reports non_canonical_writer, structural_memory_auto_write, test_evidence_provenance_not_found, and unbound_memory warnings.
entries.rationale: The warnings are visible in the receipt, but this slice found no direct evidence that this specific receipt changed a later decision.
entries.claim_ceiling: Current seed marks this latest receipt warning set as noisy, not globally useless.

entries.observed_output: Lexical mock-only and production-derived expected-value scans can match test fixtures that intentionally contain weak-signal strings.
entries.rationale: The tool's own test file contains deliberate weak-signal strings; this proves lexical signals need reviewer context and must not become gates.
entries.claim_ceiling: This marks the lexical signal as weak reviewer aid, not useless.

entries.observed_output: The current seed is output-fed; defenses that never produce output cannot appear as zombie candidates without a full inventory comparison.
entries.rationale: Zombie detection requires comparing the full defense inventory against observed outputs. This seed records that the required inventory line has not run.
entries.claim_ceiling: No zombie defense is retired or even classified by this seed.

entries.observed_output: Existing code already separates signal_class from decision_effect for change-control promotion logic.
entries.rationale: The vocabulary should be reused by the ledger design, but this seed did not find recent memory evidence that the tool output itself changed a session decision.
entries.claim_ceiling: Vocabulary reuse candidate only; not proof that change_control_summary is currently decision-changing.

summary.first_followup: Add a read-only inventory-line pass before classifying any zombie or retire_candidate defense.
summary.second_followup: Review whether noisy closeout warning codes should be baseline-aggregated instead of surfaced as high-salience per-session warnings.
summary.third_followup: Run a wider ledger pass over more than one week before retiring or downgrading any defense.
